Ms. Chang, appearing via Zoom, stated that she has been unable to reach Mr. Bresler's office and has been waiting for the final accounting to be processed so that distributions to the beneficiaries can be made.

The Court noted that the remaining issues in the case are minimal and could be easily resolved. Ms. Cheng confirmed that the main pending issue involved a request from the examiner regarding the extraordinary fees, and she had been working with Mr. Bresler's assistant on the accounting. Ms. Cheng also addressed a $13,000 rent write-off, explaining that it was part of a settlement agreement due to the decedent's spouse receiving rent funds after the building sale. Ms. Cheng confirmed that all necessary taxes have been accounted for. Regarding the $7,200 in extraordinary attorney fees, Ms. Chang acknowledged the work done related to the sale of the property and indicated she had no objection to the request.

The court determined that the remaining issues were minimal and stated its intention to waive the defects in the petition. The Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant, subject to modification of the submitted proposed order.
